description: Fix hamburger menu button not functioning on mobile and ensure profile photo displays PJ1.jpg correctly with proper object-position.
steps: In styles.css - Update .menu-toggle default styles to add cursor: pointer, flex-direction: column, align-items: center, justify-content: center, gap: 4px, padding: 0
In styles.css - In media query @media (max-width: 1040px), change .menu-toggle { display: block; } to .menu-toggle { display: flex; }
In styles.css - Remove margin: 4px auto from .menu-toggle span since gap handles spacing
In styles.css - Add object-position: top to .profile-panel img so the face is visible in the square crop
In index.html - Update the img width and height attributes to match the actual PJ1.jpg dimensions (760x1280)
acceptance_criteria: The .menu-toggle button has cursor: pointer and flex layout properties
The media query shows .menu-toggle { display: flex; } instead of display: block
The .menu-toggle span no longer has margin: 4px auto
The profile img tag uses width=760 height=1280 matching PJ1.jpg actual dimensions
verification: Run: grep -n 'cursor: pointer' stella/styles.css (should show in menu-toggle rule)
Run: grep -n 'display: flex' stella/styles.css (should show in menu-toggle media query rule)
Run: grep -n 'object-position' stella/styles.css (should show in profile-panel img rule)
Run: grep -n 'width="760"' stella/index.html (should show in profile img)
findings: Hamburger menu was not displaying lines because .menu-toggle used display:none/block without flex layout - the spans had no way to position themselves. Changed to flex with column direction and gap. Also fixed profile image cropping by adding object-position:top so face is visible in square crop.
